item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A consistently opposes the statement, emphasizing individual autonomy, anti-oppression safeguards, transparency, and minority voices while allowing proportionate public-interest restrictions. Agent B consistently supports some society-priority in public safety and health contexts while repeatedly adding rights/accountability limits. Both agents engage specific examples from the other side and move only modestly toward balance; no full flip is visible.
